name: Pipeline-Reframing (视觉系统)
full_intro_template: Paragraph 1 (Task + application): <Task> is a core building block for <application>. In realistic environments, however, <occlusion/clutter/domain shift> makes robust estimation difficult.

Paragraph 2 (Current pipeline taxonomy): Existing methods can be broadly categorized into <family A> and <family B>. While effective in controlled settings, both families break down when <failure condition>.
Paragraph 3 (Failure diagnosis): The root cause is that current pipelines attempt to solve <coupled hard subproblems> simultaneously, which amplifies errors in <critical intermediate variable>.

Paragraph 4 (Reframed pipeline): We propose to decompose the problem into two stages: first <focus stage>, then <matching/refinement stage>. This decomposition converts one hard global problem into multiple tractable local problems.
Paragraph 5 (Method sketch): Concretely, our model uses <module 1> to generate <proposal>, and <module 2> to estimate <pair-wise relation/pose> with <mask/attention mechanism>.
Paragraph 6 (Experimental evidence): Experiments on <benchmark A/B> show state-of-the-art performance, with clear gains in <metric>. Ablation confirms each module contributes to robustness.
Paragraph 7 (Contributions): We contribute (i) a new decomposition perspective, (ii) an effective two-stage architecture, and (iii) strong empirical validation on challenging scenes.
abstract_template: P3: New decomposition pipeline and key modules.
P4: Quantitative improvements and robustness claim.
sentence_stems: We first focus on ..., and then estimate ... for each proposal.
The core idea is to decompose one-to-many matching into multiple pair-wise subproblems.
This decomposition provides a practical and robust alternative to global matching.
